notes: Do not rename style_ids once frozen. Add new IDs only; never reuse retired ones.

styles.name: Buzz Cut
styles.description: Hair clipped to a single short uniform length all over, typically under 6mm, with no taper or fade.

styles.name: Crew Cut
styles.description: Short military-inspired cut with slightly longer top than sides, blended short on the sides but not faded to skin.

styles.name: Caesar Cut
styles.description: Short, even-length cut with a short straight fringe combed forward onto the forehead.

styles.name: French Crop
styles.description: Short cut with a forward-swept top and a defined blunt fringe; typically paired with tapered or faded sides.

styles.name: Textured Crop
styles.description: Short cropped top with visible piecey texture, messy fringe, usually paired with a fade or taper.

styles.name: Edgar Cut
styles.description: Blunt straight-line fringe cut low on the forehead with very short sides, often faded; heavy flat fringe is the signature.

styles.name: Ivy League
styles.description: Longer version of the crew cut with enough top length to sweep to the side; collegiate, groomed look.

styles.name: Side Part
styles.description: Classic gentleman's cut with a hard or soft parting on one side and top combed across.

styles.name: Comb Over
styles.description: Top swept across and over to one side with a distinct part; modern comb over is paired with short faded sides.

styles.name: Slick Back
styles.description: Medium-to-long top combed straight back from the forehead with no part, often with product for sheen.

styles.name: Pompadour
styles.description: Front hair swept up and back into a pronounced volume above the forehead; modern pompadour often paired with fade.

styles.name: Quiff
styles.description: Front section lifted and swept back with textured volume; shorter and less sculpted than a pompadour.

styles.name: Faux Hawk
styles.description: Hair on top styled upward into a central ridge, with shorter but not shaved sides; mohawk silhouette without the shave.

styles.name: Mohawk
styles.description: Strip of longer hair down the center of the head with sides shaved to skin or very short.

styles.name: Undercut
styles.description: Sharp length contrast — long top with disconnected uniformly very-short sides (not blended as a fade).

styles.name: Low Taper
styles.description: Gradual shortening of hair that begins just above the ears and neckline; no skin exposure.

styles.name: Mid Taper
styles.description: Gradual shortening that starts around the temples/mid-side; shorter than low taper but not reaching the skin.

styles.name: High Taper
styles.description: Taper that starts high on the head above the temples; dramatic length contrast without skin exposure.

styles.name: Low Fade
styles.description: Sides faded to skin starting low near the ears/neckline; short fade zone.

styles.name: Mid Fade
styles.description: Sides faded to skin starting around the temples/mid-side; balanced fade zone.

styles.name: High Fade
styles.description: Sides faded to skin starting high above the temples; tall bald zone.

styles.name: Skin Fade
styles.description: Any fade that blends all the way down to bare skin at its shortest point; often full-body shaved look at the base.

styles.name: Burst Fade
styles.description: Semi-circular fade curving around the ear with length preserved at the neckline behind it.

styles.name: Drop Fade
styles.description: Fade that dips lower behind the ear than at the temple, following a curved 'dropped' line.

styles.name: Temple Fade
styles.description: Localized fade only at the temples/sideburns area; rest of the sides keep length.

styles.name: Bald
styles.description: Fully shaved or naturally hairless head with no visible hair length.

styles.name: Curtains
styles.description: Medium-length hair with a center part that frames the face on both sides like drapes.

styles.name: Flow
styles.description: Medium-to-long hair brushed back with natural wave, often ear-length or longer; associated with athletic/surfer look.

styles.name: Long Layered Hair
styles.description: Long hair with layers cut throughout to add movement; no strong silhouette change.

styles.name: Shag
styles.description: Choppy layered cut with lots of movement, wispy ends, and curtain bangs or fringe; heavy texture on top.

styles.name: Wolf Cut
styles.description: Hybrid of shag and mullet: voluminous layered crown, wispy face-framing, and longer lengths at the back.

styles.name: Mullet
styles.description: Short on the top and sides, distinctly longer at the back; the length contrast front-to-back is the signature.

styles.name: Bob
styles.description: Chin-length cut that falls evenly around the head, with a straight or slightly angled bottom line.

styles.name: Long Bob
styles.description: Shoulder-grazing bob, longer than a classic bob but with the same blunt silhouette.

styles.name: Asymmetric Bob
styles.description: Bob cut noticeably longer on one side than the other.

styles.name: Pixie
styles.description: Very short cut close to the head with short sides and a slightly longer top, often with fringe.

styles.name: Bixie
styles.description: Hybrid of bob and pixie: longer than a pixie at the crown but shorter than a chin-length bob.

styles.name: Blunt Cut
styles.description: All hair cut to a single straight length at the bottom with no layering; sharp perimeter line.

styles.name: Shoulder-Length Layered
styles.description: Hair that falls at the shoulders with soft layering; general mid-length look.

styles.name: Afro
styles.description: Natural tightly-coiled hair worn out at full volume in a rounded silhouette.

styles.name: Afro Taper
styles.description: Afro with tapered or faded sides, leaving volume on top.

styles.name: Twist Out
styles.description: Natural hair set in two-strand twists and unraveled to leave defined springy coils.

styles.name: Bantu Knots
styles.description: Small coiled knots of twisted hair arranged across the scalp in a pattern.

styles.name: Locs
styles.description: Rope-like matted strands of hair formed through coiling/twisting, worn at any length.

styles.name: Box Braids
styles.description: Three-strand braids sectioned in square parts across the scalp, typically with extensions.

styles.name: Knotless Braids
styles.description: Box braids started without a knot at the root; smoother and flatter to the scalp.

styles.name: Cornrows
styles.description: Braids plaited flat against the scalp in straight or curved rows.

styles.name: Two-Strand Twists
styles.description: Pairs of hair sections wrapped around each other from root to tip, worn as-is (not unraveled).

styles.name: Ponytail
styles.description: Hair gathered and tied at the back or top of the head with a single secured tail hanging down.

styles.name: Top Knot
styles.description: Hair gathered and wrapped into a single knot or bun at the very top of the head.
